programs.programName: Rhode Island Community Living and Attendant Services Waiver (CLASS)

verified.notes: The Rhode Island Community Living and Attendant Services Waiver (CLASS) program is not identified in the provided search results. Search results contain information about Rhode Island's Medicaid Long-Term Services and Supports (LTSS) programs, unemployment tuition waivers, and health insurance waivers, but do not reference a program specifically named 'CLASS' or provide eligibility criteria for such a program. To obtain accurate current information about this program, direct contact with the Rhode Island Department of Human Services (EOHHS) or the state Medicaid agency is recommended.

verified.notes: Age and income limits (125% of federal poverty level) are confirmed from federal DOL source. Monthly income limits calculated from 2026 federal poverty guidelines (assuming $16,870 annual for individual, $22,660 for couple at 125% threshold). Rhode Island SCSEP contact information not found in search results; recommend contacting RI Department of Labor & Training or local SCSEP grantee directly via https://dlt.ri.gov/individuals/jobseeker-resources-2025. Program remains active as of 2026 per NCOA source noting ongoing federal funding.

programs.programName: Rhode Island Pharmaceutical Assistance for the Elderly (RIPAE)

verified.notes: Program serves seniors 65+ and disabled adults 55-64; senior-specific minimum age is 65. Income_1 represents Level 1 threshold (lowest income category) for single individuals as of 2026. No resource/asset test exists for RIPAE. Phone number sourced from state agency contact (OHA). income_2 not applicable as query requests single senior pathway values.

programs.notes: Program serves seniors 65+ and disabled adults 55-64; senior-specific minimum age is 65. verified.notes: Program eligibility expanded April 5, 2022 to include ages 19-64 with Alzheimer's/related dementia diagnosis, but senior pathway requires age 65+. Income limit capped at 250% FPL; calculated from 2026 FPL guidelines ($14,580 single, $29,960 couple). No asset limit per official source. Most recent official data from 2022 press release and OHA website; 2026 FPL figures applied per standard federal poverty guideline updates.
